name: Chocolate Protein Donut Holes
description: Glazed chocolate protein timbits, synthesized from three source recipes (a 45-ball cookies&cream air-fryer dough, a 26-ball oxywhey air-fryer dough, and an 8-ball baked egg+pudding dough). Best-of: the EGG + a little cornstarch from the baked version (the anti-rubber, anti-dry levers - fat for tenderness, starch to tenderize the crumb and hold moisture; cornstarch stands in for the SF pudding mix so there's nothing to buy), the real cocoa level + Greek-yogurt body + quick air-fry from the air-fryer versions (chocolatey and hard to overbake), AP flour + baking powder instead of drying coconut flour, and just ONE scoop of protein (two scoops is what turns these rubbery - more protein powder is NOT free here). Chocolate protein (out of vanilla); all the chocolate lives in the timbit. The glaze is a separate reusable sub-recipe (Reduced-Sugar Vanilla Donut Glaze) - real sugar at half the usual amount + monk fruit, so it sets to a true Tim-Hortons crust while staying light (fat-free carbs); flavor-neutral so it tops any timbit flavor. ~16 balls, very low fat/ball, high protein.

steps.instructionText: Whisk together the dry ingredients: AP flour, chocolate protein powder, cocoa, cornstarch, baking powder (1 tsp), granulated Splenda, and a pinch of salt.
Add the Greek yogurt and the whole egg; stir and fold to a soft, slightly sticky dough. Chill 15-20 min until it firms up enough to handle (protein/yogurt dough is sticky warm).
Roll into ~16 even balls with damp or lightly cocoa-dusted hands.

Air fry at 350F for ~5-6 min (or bake at 350F for 10-12 min) until just set and springy to a light press. Pull them the moment they spring back - do NOT chase a dry center, that's where they turn rubbery. Cool ~10 min.

Make a batch of the glaze (see sub-recipe). Dip or toss the donut holes in it while they're warm but NOT hot - warm grabs the glaze, hot makes it slide off. Set on a wire rack and AIR-DRY at room temp (NOT the fridge - cold keeps a sugar glaze tacky) until it skins over, ~15-20 min; double-dip for a thicker, crackly Tim-Hortons-style shell. Expect some to drip; that's normal.

notes.text: Overbaking is the #1 thing that ruins these. Protein-powder + Greek-yogurt dough goes rubbery and dry the second it overcooks. Air fryers run hot and fast - start checking at 5 min and pull the moment they spring back to a light press; the center can look slightly underdone and it'll be perfect once cooled.

The egg, the cornstarch, and holding protein to ONE scoop are the anti-rubber levers. The egg adds fat for tenderness, the cornstarch tenderizes the crumb and holds moisture (it replaces the SF pudding mix - same job, nothing to buy), and one scoop of protein keeps it from going dry and bouncy. Don't add a second scoop to 'boost macros' - extra whey is exactly what turns protein bakes rubbery. Want more protein? Eat two.
Chill the dough 15-20 min before rolling - warm protein/yogurt dough is sticky and won't ball up cleanly. Damp or lightly cocoa-dusted hands keep it from gluing to you.
